steps.title: Spot the exception
steps.content: The first sentence gives a general possibility: a successful debut novelist may struggle with a follow-up. The next sentence says Tommy Orange's follow-up elevated his reputation. The transition must show that this is a possible exception to the usual concern.

steps.title: Reject example signals
steps.content: Choice A and Choice D make Orange's case sound like a direct illustration of the struggle, but his sequel does the opposite. Choice B signals a result, as if success should naturally cause an elevated reputation. The needed transition introduces a case that can occur despite the general risk. The answer is C.
